title: gh#42: Phase 11 follow-up: calendar today-cell highlighting regression test
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/42.
Backlog section: Tests.

## Context

Phase 11 UAT (test 16) confirmed the M/W/D view-switch and the custom shadcn-Tabs toolbar work correctly. Today's cell visibly highlights via react-big-calendar's built-in `.rbc-today` CSS class.

What's missing is an **app-level regression test**. If a future refactor changes the toolbar or replaces react-big-calendar, the `.rbc-today` styling could disappear silently — there is no test asserting that today's cell carries a today-marker class or distinguishable styling hook.

## Acceptance criteria

- Vitest case in `packages/web/src/pages/calendar/__tests__/CalendarPage.test.tsx`:
  1. Mock today's date to a deterministic value
  2. Render `<CalendarPage />`
  3. Assert the rendered DOM has at least one element with `rbc-today` (or whatever class denotes today after our wrapper) on the date cell that matches today
- Test passes with the current implementation; documents the contract for future toolbar changes.

## Why now

Discovered during Phase 11 UAT (see `.planning/phases/11-snippets-search-calendar-polish/11-UAT.md` test 16 follow-up note). Non-blocking — visual highlight works in production.
reproduction:

What's missing is an **app-level regression test**. If a future refactor changes the toolbar or replaces react-big-calendar, the `.rbc-today` styling could disappear silently — there is no test asserting that today's cell carries a today-marker class or distinguishable styling hook.
recommendation: Implement the fix described by GitHub issue gh#42 and satisfy its acceptance criteria.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.
